hypothesis: Among the Si–X alloy candidates identified by the AI framework as exhibiting invariant Li⁺ migration channel dimensions across lithiation states, those incorporating elements with high bulk modulus (e.g., W, Mo, or Re) as the alloying component X will demonstrate a statistically significant negative correlation between the alloying element's bulk modulus and the slope of channel radius versus state-of-charge, such that substituting even 5–10 at% of Si with these stiff elements mechanically pins the Si sublattice geometry and reduces volumetric expansion below 50% while maintaining gravimetric capacity above 1500 mAh/g—a relationship that can be tested by computing channel-radius slopes and volume changes via CHGNet relaxation across a systematically varied series of Li–Si–X structures drawn from the Materials Project database.
